title: Switch Overstory default agent runtime from Claude to Codex
Implement SCBS packaging and exchange workflows
description: Implement the SCBS bundle packaging, import/export, and interoperability flows described in SCBS.md. Acceptance: bundle creation and consumption commands are implemented with validation and tests, and the work stays isolated to packaging/exchange areas.
closeReason: Implemented CLI bundle export/import/handoff workflows with protocol-aligned payloads and passing tests, lint, and typecheck.

title: Build SCBS CLI foundation from spec
description: Implement the Shared Context Bundle System CLI according to SCBS.md. Deliver the core command surface, data model, storage layout, and tests needed for a usable first version. Acceptance: CLI scaffolding exists, core workflows from the spec are implemented, and leads can decompose the work into concrete implementation and validation tasks.
closeReason: Recovered the existing SCBS CLI foundation implementation onto the merge branch, validated with bun test, bun run lint, and bun run typecheck, and added repo ignores to keep the worktree clean after install.

title: Fix Overstory child-agent shared writable dirs for Seeds/Mulch/Canopy
description: Child Codex agents already run with codex --full-auto, but they still hit host-level approval prompts when commands like sd close, mulch record/sync, and cn sync write to canonical shared stores outside the worktree. Update Overstory's shared writable directory resolution so interactive child agents can write the required project-level state directories without escalation. Acceptance: child agents can run Seeds/Mulch/Canopy close/sync flows from worktrees without approval prompts caused by missing shared writable dirs, and the change is validated against the current codex runtime path.
closeReason: Resolved locally by the active Overstory install used by this repo: child-agent shared writable dirs now include .seeds, .mulch, and .canopy.

title: Route Overstory writable-dir fix to upstream overstory repo
description: scbs-f21c is blocked because the implementation point is in the installed @os-eco/overstory-cli source tree, not in /home/jarip/projects/scbs. Acceptance: capture the validated source location, hand off the fix to the Overstory repo/worktree, and keep scbs-f21c blocked here until an upstream branch or merged fix is available.
closeReason: Obsolete after consolidating to a single patched Bun-based Overstory install; no separate upstream routing work remains in this repo state.

title: Refresh project documentation
Fix failing test suite
Fix coordinator checkpoint recovery path mismatch
closeReason: Updated coordinator recovery guidance to use .overstory/agents/coordinator/identity.yaml plus shared .overstory runtime state instead of a nonexistent checkpoint path; verified with tests, lint, and typecheck.

title: Persist SCBS CLI artifact state with a local durable adapter
closeReason: Persisted SCBS CLI artifact state with a local durable JSON adapter, added storage helpers, and passed tests/lint/typecheck.

title: Recover durable-store merge handoff
description: The durable-store workstream for branch overstory/scbs-durable-store-lead/scbs-7f0a was closed on worker_done without an explicit merge_ready handoff, leaving the branch unmerged. Recover the workstream by reviewing the existing branch/worktree, confirming whether the implementation is merge-ready, and sending explicit merge_ready with branch and taskId if verified. If verification fails or more implementation is required, send an escalation with concrete blockers instead of closing the issue.
closeReason: Recovered manually: durable-store implementation already landed on master at 10ca7fd; original branch overstory/scbs-durable-store-lead/scbs-7f0a is stale and should not be merged.

title: Fix Overstory Codex agents stalling at interactive prompts
description: Codex-backed coordinator and lead agents can launch and execute initial commands, but then stop at an interactive prompt instead of autonomously finishing the coordination loop. In this session the coordinator remained at a prompt after dispatching scbs-bef6, and the recovery lead remained at a prompt after sending its error mail, requiring manual tracker/runtime cleanup. Acceptance: Codex agents continue processing mail/state or exit cleanly without waiting for terminal input after a turn completes.

title: Implement SCBS core engine gaps
description: Implement the remaining SCBS core engine work described in SCBS.md inside packages/* without depending on Mission Control internals. Focus on deterministic repository truth extraction, anchored claims/views, freshness/invalidation, bundle planning, and receipt validation so the package layer becomes a real engine instead of thin stubs. Acceptance criteria: meaningful end-to-end behavior exists across the package APIs, quality gates relevant to the lead pass, and any required follow-up work is filed as Seeds issues rather than left implicit.
closeReason: Mapped tests and public contracts for core/extraction/freshness/protocol/receipts; identified current assertions, exposed APIs, and acceptance-test gaps for builders.

title: Implement SCBS product surface and API boundary
description: Implement the remaining user-facing SCBS work in apps/cli and adjacent top-level surface areas, aligned with SCBS.md. Focus on turning the current thin CLI/durable adapter into a stronger standalone product boundary, including stable API-facing behavior, operator-friendly workflows, and documentation that matches the real shipped command and service surface. Acceptance criteria: the SCBS surface is materially more usable as a standalone product entrypoint, tests/documentation are updated, and any unresolved follow-up gaps are filed in Seeds.
closeReason: Aligned the SCBS standalone surface across CLI service commands, local durable config/state docs, and tests.

title: Exclude local Claude settings from root lint
closeReason: Added a repo-level Biome ignore for .claude/settings.local.json so root lint no longer depends on local Claude settings formatting.

title: Implement a real HTTP API surface for scbs serve
closeReason: Implemented the live serve HTTP surface in apps/cli, exposed /health and /api/v1, updated serve reporting to live/listening, and added CLI/e2e coverage with all gates passing.

title: Implement SCBS HTTP API endpoints for bundles, freshness, and receipts
description: Extend the live SCBS serve HTTP boundary beyond /health and the root index so it exposes a meaningful API-first surface aligned with SCBS.md. Focus on the apps/cli HTTP entrypoint and adjacent service wiring for bundle planning, bundle inspection/freshness, freshness status/impacts, and receipt submission/listing using the existing core capabilities where practical. Acceptance criteria: the serve process exposes real /api/v1 endpoints for the primary SCBS workflows, request/response behavior is covered by tests, and any intentionally deferred API gaps are captured as follow-up Seeds issues rather than left implicit.

title: Implement parent-child bundle inheritance
description: SCBS.md requires parent/child agent handoff via task bundles, but the current implementation only stores parentBundleId in bundle metadata and does not inherit prior file scope, views, claims, commands, or freshness into child bundle planning. Implement the next MVP slice for bundle inheritance so child bundle requests can reuse parent context in a deterministic, freshness-aware way. Acceptance: bundle planning uses an existing parent bundle when provided, inheritance behavior is explicit and tested, CLI/API surfaces preserve the contract, and regression coverage proves handoff works without duplicating stale or unrelated context.

title: Add multi-repo bundle composition coverage
description: SCBS.md requires multi-repo bundle composition tests, and the current repo already models BundleRequest.repoIds as an array but only exercises single-repo bundle planning in tests. Implement the next in-repo slice so bundle planning and its public surfaces are proven to work across more than one repository without leaking unrelated context. Acceptance: add or extend implementation/tests so multi-repo bundle requests select the right views/claims/commands across repositories, preserve freshness behavior, and are covered at the core and/or CLI/API layer where appropriate.

title: Fix repo-blind freshness invalidation across repos
description: Post-merge review on scbs-3eb6 found a real defect in packages/freshness/src/invalidation.ts: determineChangeImpact() matches changed paths against anchors, invalidation keys, view fileScope, bundle fileScope, and proofHandles using only filePath strings, with no repoId guard. As a result, same-path files in different repositories cross-expire, violating the multi-repo contract from SCBS.md. Acceptance: freshness invalidation becomes repo-aware so changes in one repo do not stale/expire artifacts for another repo with the same relative path, and focused regression coverage proves the defect is fixed through the freshness/core path and any necessary public surface tests.

title: Fix repo-blind freshness invalidation across repos
description: Post-merge review on scbs-3eb6 found a real defect in packages/freshness/src/invalidation.ts: determineChangeImpact() matches changed paths against anchors, invalidation keys, view fileScope, bundle fileScope, and proofHandles using only filePath strings, with no repoId guard. As a result, same-path files in different repositories cross-expire, violating the multi-repo contract from SCBS.md. Acceptance: freshness invalidation becomes repo-aware so changes in one repo do not stale/expire artifacts for another repo with the same relative path, and focused regression coverage proves the defect is fixed through the freshness/core path and any necessary public surface tests.

title: Add HTTP API parity for remaining SCBS bundle, freshness, and receipt operations
description: SCBS.md and the README describe a versioned HTTP API for bundle lifecycle, freshness recompute/status, and receipt validation/rejection, but the current repo appears to expose only a subset of the CLI surface through apps/cli HTTP handlers. Implement the next meaningful SCBS backlog slice by bringing the HTTP contract closer to the documented CLI surface without touching local-user state files. Acceptance: the HTTP service exposes the missing bundle/receipt/freshness endpoints needed for parity with the implemented service layer, returns consistent JSON/error behavior, and includes automated coverage proving the new endpoints work end-to-end.

title: Complete remaining SCBS.md delivery gaps
description: Drive the repository from its current local-json MVP toward the concrete deliverables described in SCBS.md: full versioned API coverage, durable PostgreSQL-backed storage and migrations, explicit server/OpenAPI artifacts, official SDK and integration adapters, and verification/CI coverage that proves the external contract. Acceptance: the remaining SCBS.md gaps are decomposed into mergeable workstreams with dependencies tracked, and each stream lands in the correct order without relying on tracker closure as proof of implementation.
closeReason: All dependent SCBS.md completion streams merged: scbs-cc80, scbs-bbc8, scbs-fc56, scbs-0ed0, scbs-70d6

title: Ship the SCBS server and OpenAPI contract surface
description: Implement the API-first server surface described in SCBS.md, including the missing versioned routes, server package boundary, and OpenAPI contract artifacts. Scope: apps/server, openapi assets, and route/service wiring needed to expose the documented API contract. Acceptance: the server surface exists as a first-class package with the documented API endpoints and contract artifacts, and it can integrate with the durable backend without overlapping the current apps/cli HTTP parity patch.

title: Implement PostgreSQL storage, config, and migration pipeline for SCBS
description: Replace the current local-json-only durable path with the PostgreSQL-backed storage model described in SCBS.md. Scope: durable storage adapters, migration application flow, config shape, and persistence semantics under packages/core storage plus config and migrations. Acceptance: SCBS can persist through a PostgreSQL-backed path with versioned migrations and compatibility with the protocol data model, without overlapping apps/cli HTTP parity work.

title: Add official SCBS SDK, views package, and integration adapters
description: Bring the package surface closer to SCBS.md by adding the official SDK/client layer, the views package boundary, and the SISU/Mission Control adapter packages. Scope: packages/sdk, packages/views, packages/adapter-sisu, and packages/adapter-mission-control. Acceptance: consumers have explicit package entrypoints for the SCBS client contract and integration mapping, aligned to the server/protocol surface rather than only internal core helpers.

title: Add SCBS contract verification, PostgreSQL integration tests, and CI checks
description: Close the remaining verification gap against SCBS.md by wiring root scripts, CI, migration checks, OpenAPI checks, and PostgreSQL-backed integration coverage. Scope: root package scripts, workflow/config/test harness wiring, and the final verification surfaces needed to prove the external contract. Acceptance: CI exercises lint, typecheck, unit tests, PostgreSQL integration tests, migration checks, and OpenAPI contract checks in a way that matches the intended SCBS delivery model.

title: Enforce bundle cache expiry and add verification coverage
closeReason: Superseded by scbs-1c55, merged to origin/master at 32bd35f

title: Enforce bundle cache expiry semantics and add verification coverage
description: Implement the next SCBS 1.0 gap as a single focused lane. packages/core/src/cache/service.ts persists expiresAt on cache entries but does not enforce expiry on reads, so expired bundle cache entries can still be returned. Scope: enforce expiry semantics in the cache/service path and add tests proving expired entries are not served and cache freshness/lookup behavior respects expiry. Acceptance: expired cache entries are treated as misses/invalid, non-expired entries still work, and automated coverage proves the behavior without opening unrelated SCBS workstreams.

title: Implement PostgreSQL-backed recompute jobs for freshness updates
closeReason: Superseded by published scbs-14dd merge at 4326eb1

title: Implement PostgreSQL-backed recompute jobs for freshness updates
description: Implement the next SCBS Phase 3 gap as a single focused lane. SCBS.md Phase 3 calls for background freshness workers, change events, targeted recompute, and PostgreSQL-backed jobs, but the current repo only computes selective recompute plans in memory and exposes synchronous recompute/expire paths. Scope: add a durable PostgreSQL-backed recompute job path for freshness updates, with the minimum surrounding wiring and verification needed to prove the durable job flow without opening unrelated SCBS workstreams. Acceptance: recompute work can be enqueued and driven through a PostgreSQL-backed durable path, targeted freshness recompute remains coherent, and automated coverage proves the new job behavior.

title: Add background worker to drain PostgreSQL freshness recompute jobs
closeReason: Superseded by scbs-dd7d single active Phase 3 worker task

title: Add bounded background worker path for PostgreSQL freshness recompute jobs
description: Implement the next SCBS Phase 3 gap as a single focused lane. Durable PostgreSQL-backed freshness recompute jobs now exist, but SCBS.md Phase 3 still calls for background freshness workers and change-driven processing rather than only manual synchronous draining. Scope: add a bounded worker/runner path that drains PostgreSQL freshness recompute jobs asynchronously through the existing durable freshness/core/service seam, with the minimum service/CLI wiring and focused tests needed to prove coherent asynchronous processing. Acceptance: recompute jobs can be drained by a bounded worker path without introducing unrelated orchestration surfaces, targeted freshness behavior remains coherent, and automated coverage proves the asynchronous drain behavior.

title: Promote validated receipts into durable claims and views
Promote validated receipts into durable claims and views
description: Implement the next SCBS task as a single focused lane. Make PostgreSQL/durable receipt validation promote the validated claim, rebuild affected views so durable behavior matches core receipt lifecycle semantics, and add focused tests. Scope is limited to the durable receipt validation/claim/view path and directly related tests only. Acceptance: durable receipt validation promotes validated claims and updates affected views coherently, focused automated coverage proves the behavior, the branch merges and is pushed before any additional SCBS task starts.

title: Promote validated receipts in PostgreSQL durable mode
Promote validated receipts in PostgreSQL durable service
Rebase PostgreSQL durable receipt promotion fix onto current master
closeReason: Superseded: current master no longer contains the PostgreSQL durable-service surface assumed by this rebase task

title: Reintroduce PostgreSQL durable-service receipt promotion on current master
description: Retarget the stale scbs-7735 lane to the real current-master gap. Current origin/master no longer contains the PostgreSQL durable-service path assumed by scbs-7735, so a small rebase fix is not possible. Scope: reintroduce the minimum PostgreSQL durable-service support on current master needed for validateReceipt to promote validated claims into durable claims, rebuild affected views coherently, preserve reject semantics, and add focused verification for the PostgreSQL/durable path. Acceptance: the new implementation lands cleanly on current master without regressing published receipt behavior, verification proves the durable PostgreSQL promotion path, and no parallel SCBS workstreams are opened.

title: Reintroduce PostgreSQL durable receipt validation and promotion on current master
Add claims and views HTTP endpoints to the live SCBS API
Add SISU integration endpoints for bundle requests and receipts
Add Mission Control repo-sync integration endpoint to the SCBS API
Extend the SCBS SDK with claims, views, and integration endpoint clients
Implement a real PostgreSQL runtime for the SCBS service
description: SCBS.md defines PostgreSQL as the primary durable store, but the shipped runtime is still hard-coded to local-json in apps/cli and apps/server surface types/config. Implement an actual PostgreSQL-backed runtime path that the CLI/service can boot and use for repository, claim, view, bundle, cache, and receipt persistence instead of only verifying schema creation. Acceptance: service config can select PostgreSQL, the runtime executes against PostgreSQL for core read/write flows, local-json remains an explicit dev mode if retained, and focused automated coverage proves the real durable path works end-to-end.

title: Add durable freshness events, recompute jobs, and worker execution
description: SCBS.md Phase 3 calls for change events, targeted recompute, background workers, and durable job state, but the current source still computes freshness updates synchronously in memory and contains no real job/outbox worker path. Add durable freshness event capture, recompute job persistence, and a bounded worker that drains jobs against the durable store. Acceptance: repo-change reporting enqueues durable recompute work, worker execution updates claims/views/bundles coherently, job state survives process restarts, and coverage proves the event-to-recompute path.

title: Implement the missing anchored claim graph and richer derived views
description: Current extraction and derivation stop at file hash facts, exported symbols, package commands, file-scope views, and command_workflow views. SCBS.md requires an anchored claim graph with repository facts, symbols, dependency edges, richer claim classes, and reusable subsystem/interface/workflow/decision views. Acceptance: core storage/runtime preserves symbols and graph edges, derivation uses that graph to build richer claims and view types, and tests prove the new artifacts remain anchored, freshness-aware, and explainable.

title: Make bundle planning dependency-aware and enforce planner constraints
description: The current planner does deterministic selection and parent inheritance, but it does not expand dependency neighborhoods, enforce token budgets, or resolve commands/tests from dependency-aware context the way SCBS.md specifies. Upgrade the planner to follow the documented order of operations: scoped repo resolution, dependency expansion, candidate view selection, critical claim selection, freshness filtering, command/test resolution, token-budget enforcement, cache reuse, and explainable assembly. Acceptance: planner outputs remain deterministic, dependency-aware selection is covered by tests, and bundle diagnostics explain why artifacts were included or excluded.

title: Align the standalone service boundary with real runtime modes and contract truth
description: The repo now ships a CLI, server package, SDK, and OpenAPI artifacts, but the public surface still advertises a local-json-only runtime and a simplified contract that does not yet reflect the intended durable standalone product. Align config, CLI/server status types, doctor/serve output, and generated OpenAPI artifacts with the actual supported runtime modes and operational semantics. Acceptance: public service metadata and contract artifacts describe the real product honestly, runtime capabilities are mode-aware, and operator-facing docs/status output no longer imply features that are not actually implemented.
